titleStmt.title: Capitula Sapientiae in Boulogne, BM, MS 2 (2)

publicationStmt.p: Digital editions of summaries in Medieval Bibles

bibl.author: Donatien de Bruyne
bibl.title: Sommaires, divisions et rubriques de la Bible latine

bibl.editor: Monachi abbatiae pontificiae Sancti Hieronymi in Urbe
bibl.title: Biblia Sacra iuxta Latinam vulgatam versionem ad codicum fidem... Sapientia Salomonis, Liber Hiesu filii Sirach cum praefationibus et variis capitulorum seriebus

supportDesc.extent:
II + 299 + III folios
height 512 mm width 350 mm

tagsDecl.rendition: the digital reproduction is b/w, impossible to determine color

front.p:
The series of capitula published here is unknown both to de Bruyne and to the Benedictine edition of the Vulgate, and bears no resemblance to any of the other attested series; picking up and continuing the system of sigla adopted in the Benedictine edition, we label it C_dobips. Among the manuscripts recorded in the DOBiPS database, it appears only in Bl1_2 (Boulogne, BM, MS 2 [2]), that is, the second volume of the so-called "Bible of Saint-André-au-Bois" (late 12th cent., Northern France).
Although in the manuscript the number is usually given at the end of each capitulum, here, for the sake of consistency with the other editions of the project, the number has been placed before the text. Occasionally, a capitulum is numbered twice, for example when its text extends over two lines, each concluding with a number. In these cases, both numbers have also been given before the capitulum.
The point in the text of Sapientia where Bl1_2 places the chapter number corresponding to each capitulum can be accessed either by clicking on the two-page icon next to each capitulum number, or by clicking on the "Passi paralleli" thumbnail (in the right-hand panel of the "Testo di lettura" screen).
Since the marginal chapter numbering in the text (38 chapters) does not match the table of capitula (57 titles), it is impossible to provide the correspondences within the edited text; the list of Bl1_2's chapters is therefore given below. As a comparison with de Bruyne's data (p. 480-481) shows, they generally match the chapter numbering of series A (ibid., p. 172-174) and Antipel (ibid.).
1 1:1 diligite iustitiam
2 1:5 sanctus enim spiritus
3 1:11 custodite ergo vos
4 2:1 dixerunt enim apud se
5 3:1 iustorum autem animae
6 3:10 impii autem secundum quae cogitaverunt
7 3:16 filii autem adulterorum
8 4:7 iustus autem si morte praeoccupatus
9 5:1 tunc stabunt iusti
10 5:16 iusti autem in perpetuum vivent
11 6:2 audite ergo reges
12 6:12 concupiscite ergo sermones meos
13 6:20 incorruptio autem facit
14 7:1 sum quidem et ego
15 7:7 propter hoc optavi
16 7:16 in manu enim [om. Bl1_2] illius
17 7:24 adtingit autem ubique
18 7:30 sapientiam [-a Bl1_2] autem
19 8:20 et cum essem magis [magister Bl1_2] bonus
20 9:19 et [nam Bl1_2] per sapientiam
21 10:10 haec [iustum autem Bl1_2] profugum irae fratris
22 10:15 haec populum iustum
23 11:16 quod quidam errantes colebant
24 11:23 quoniam tamquam momentum
25 12:1 bonus enim spiritus tuus [o quam bonus et suauis est domine spiritus tuus Bl1_2]
26 13:1 vani sunt autem omnes homines
27 13:10 infelices autem sunt
28 15:1 tu autem deus [domine deus Bl1_2] noster
29 15:15 quoniam omnia idola
30 16:13 tu enim vitae et mortis
31 16:16 negantes enim nosse
32 16:20 pro quibus angelorum esca
33 16:24 creatura enim tibi [tua tibi Bl1_2] factori
34 17:1 magna enim sunt iudicia tua
35 17:6 apparebat autem illis
36 18:21 properans enim homo
37 18:23 cum enim iam acervatim
38 19:12 in adlocutione [allocutionem Bl1_2] enim desiderii

item.title: De diligenda iusticia et cordis castitate
De spiritu sancto et uero qui refugit simulantes
De dictis impiorum quibus iustum impetunt
De sancta uirginitate per quam salus uenit
De beatitudine quȩ iustum post mortem sequitur

list.item: XI - XII De inani manu iniquorum reprehensione qua qu? se in iudicio coarguunt

item.title: De eterna uita iustorum et qualitate iudicii
Qualiter agente sapientia iacob euaserit iram fratris

app.note: The final part of the number (VI) is not visible in the digitisation due to the tight binding of the manuscript and should therefore be understood only as a plausible integration.

item.title: Qualiter per eandem sapientiam gens iustorum sit saluata

app.note: The final part of the number (I) is not visible in the digitisation due to the tight binding of the manuscript and should therefore be understood only as a plausible integration.

item.title: Qualiter per sapientiam ioseph exemptus sit
Qualiter per sapientiam hostes superati sint
Quod per quȩ peccat quis per hȩc et torqueatur
Quod non odiendo iram infert deus sed corripiendo
Moderatio The rubricated initial was not executed. iudiciorum dei
Qualiter iniquis in suis studiis penas attribuit
Conuincit eos potuisse deum scire per ea quȩ facta sunt
Quȩ res homines in errorem induxit
Quod in ira deus misericordiȩ recordetur

list.item: XXXIX - XL Quod non herbis aut humanis medicaminibus sed solo dei uerbo curati sunt qui saluabantur

item.title: Qualiter iudicium dei hominum condempnet malitiam
De terribilibus portentis quibus territi sunt increduli
De salute iustorum et nece impiorum
De uirtute sacrificii qua ira dei compressa est
